title: Mounting clip for networked led lighting system
abstract: A clip for mounting a linear LED lamp to an overhead dropped ceiling grid or to a planar support surface. The mounting clip has a pair of spaced resilient fingers extending downwardly and defining an interior region between the fingers sized to receive a portion of the linear LED lamp heat sink. Each finger has an internally facing engagement portion comprising protrusions configured to interlock with one or more external ridges of the heat sink of a linear LED lamp to securely retain the linear LED lamp between the fingers. In one form, the mounting clip has upper tab portions configured to engage and connect to the overhead dropped ceiling grid. In another form, the mounting clip is adapted to be secured to a planar support by means of a mechanical fastener or magnetically. The disclosed mounting clip can also be configured to secure a network cable.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A mounting clip for use in securing a linear LED lamp to an overhead dropped ceiling grid, the linear LED lamp comprising an elongate body extending along a first axis between spaced first and second ends and comprising an elongate heat sink formed of a heat dissipating material and comprising ridges disposed on the outer surface thereof and extending along the first axis, and at least one LED emitter panel secured to the heat sink, the mounting clip comprising: a planar mid-portion having an upper surface adapted to oppose the lower surface of a horizontally extending ledge of a channel member of the overhead dropped ceiling grid; first and second tabs extending upwardly from opposite ends of the mid-portion and comprising respective flanges projecting inwardly toward each other; the first and second flanges having respective first and second surfaces, the first and second tabs configured so that the first and second surfaces can be placed in confronting relationship with third and fourth upward facing surfaces of the ledge with the ledge disposed between the tabs to prevent separation of the mounting clip and the channel member as an incident of the mounting clip moving relative to the channel member from a position below and fully separated from the channel member upwardly into an engaged position; and a pair of spaced resilient fingers extending downwardly from opposite ends of the mid-portion and defining an interior region between the fingers sized to receive a portion of the linear LED lamp heat sink; each finger including a distal end portion comprising an internally facing engagement portion comprising protrusions configured to interlock with one or more of the ridges of the lamp heat sink to thereby securely retain the linear LED lamp between the fingers. 2. The mounting clip according to claim 1, wherein the tab portions are configured so that the flanges thereof move against lower outer edge portions of the ledge as the mounting clip is moved upwardly towards the engaged position thereby causing the tab portions to deflect outwardly to an expanded orientation, and the tabs return to a non- expanded orientation with the ledge disposed between the tabs in the engaged position. 3. The mounting clip according to claim 2, wherein the flanges comprise respective upward facing ramped portions configured to engage the lower outer edge portions of the ledge. 4. The mounting clip according to claim 3, wherein engagement of the ramped portions against the lower outer edge portions of the ledge causes the tab portions to deflect outwardly to the expanded orientation. 5. The mounting clip according to claim 2, wherein an outer surface of each of the first and second tab portions define a horizontally extending groove. 6. The mounting clip according to claim 1, wherein the fingers deflect outwardly from a relaxed orientation to an expanded assembly orientation as an incident of the linear LED lamp moving relative to the mounting clip from a position below and fully separated from the mounting clip upwardly in a path that is traverse to the length of the lamp body into an engaged position between the fingers. 7. The mounting clip according to claim 6, further comprising first and second ribs extending from the mid-portion along inner surfaces of a respective one of the resilient fingers to provide rigidity to the fingers. 8. The mounting clip according to claim 6, wherein the mounting clip is slidable along the ledge of the channel member with the mounting clip in the engaged position and the fingers in the relaxed orientation. 9. The mounting clip according to claim 8, wherein the tab portions rotate inwardly towards each other and against the ledge of the channel member to restrict sliding movement of the mounting clip as the fingers deflect outwardly to the expanded assembly orientation. 10. The mounting clip according to claim 1, wherein the fingers are configured so that the engagement portions thereof can interlock with a linear LED lamp heat sink having a generally circular circumferential geometry. 11. The mounting clip according to claim 1, wherein the fingers are configured so that the engagement portions thereof can interlock with a linear LED lamp heat sink having a generally non-circular circumferential geometry. 12. A mounting clip for use in securing a linear LED lamp to a planar support, the linear LED lamp comprising an elongate body extending along a first axis between spaced first and second ends and comprising an elongate heat sink formed of a heat dissipating material and comprising ridges disposed on the outer surface thereof and extending along the first axis, and at least one LED emitter panel secured to the heat sink, the mounting clip comprising: a planar mid-portion having an upper surface adapted to be flush mounted against the 

 planar support; and a pair of spaced resilient fingers extending downwardly from opposite ends of the mid-portion and defining an interior region between the fingers sized to receive a portion of the linear LED lamp heat sink; each finger including a distal end portion comprising an internally facing engagement portion comprising protrusions configured to interlock with one or more of the ridges of the lamp heat sink to thereby securely retain the linear LED lamp between the fingers. 13. The mounting clip according to claim 12, wherein the mid-portion includes an aperture for receiving a fastener for coupling the mounting clip to the support. 14. The mounting clip according to claim 12, wherein the support is a metal support and mid-portion includes one or more integral magnets for magnetically coupling the mounting clip to the support. 15. The mounting clip according to claim 12, wherein the fingers deflect outwardly from a relaxed orientation to an expanded assembly orientation as an incident of the linear LED lamp moving relative to the mounting clip from a position below and fully separated from the mounting clip upwardly in a path that is traverse to the length of the lamp body into an engaged position between the fingers. 16. The mounting clip according to claim 15, further comprising first and second ribs extending from the mid-portion along inner surfaces of a respective one of the resilient fingers to provide rigidity to the fingers. 17. The mounting clip according to claim 15, wherein the mounting clip is slidable along the ledge of the channel member with the mounting clip in the engaged position and the fingers in the relaxed orientation. 18. The mounting clip according to claim 17, wherein the tab portions rotate inwardly towards each other and against the ledge of the channel member to restrict slidable movement of the mounting clip as the fingers deflect outwardly to the expanded assembly orientation. 19. The mounting clip according to claim 12, wherein the fingers are configured so that the engagement portions thereof can interlock with a linear LED lamp heat sink having a generally circular circumferential geometry. 20. The mounting clip according to claim 12, wherein the fingers are configured so that the engagement portions thereof can interlock with a linear LED lamp heat sink having a generally non-circular circumferential geometry. 21. A mounting clip for use in securing a portion of a network cable to an overhead dropped ceiling grid, the mounting clip comprising: a planar mid-portion having an upper surface adapted to oppose the lower surface of a horizontally extending ledge of a channel member of the overhead dropped ceiling grid, and a lower surface on the opposite side of the mid-portion; first and second tabs extending upwardly from opposite ends of the mid-portion and comprising respective flanges projecting inwardly toward each other, the first and second flanges having respective first and second surfaces, the first and second tabs configured so that the first and second surfaces can be placed in confronting relationship with third and fourth upward facing surfaces of the ledge with the ledge disposed between the tabs to prevent separation of the mounting clip and the channel member as an incident of the mounting clip moving relative to the channel member from a position below and fully separated from the channel member upwardly into an engaged position; and a coupler disposed adjacent the lower surface of the mid-portion and configured to releasably secure the portion of the network cable to the mounting clip. 22. The mounting clip according to claim 21, wherein the tab portions are configured so that the flanges thereof move against lower outer edge portions of the ledge as the mounting clip is moved upwardly towards the engaged position thereby causing the tab portions to deflect outwardly to an expanded orientation, and the tabs return to a non- expanded orientation with the ledge disposed between the tabs in the engaged position. 23. The mounting clip according to claim 22, wherein the flanges comprise respective upward facing ramped portions configured to engage the lower outer edge portions of the ledge. 24. The mounting clip according to claim 23, wherein engagement of the ramped portions against the lower outer edge portions of the ledge causes the tab portions to deflect outwardly to the expanded orientation. 25. The mounting clip according to claim 22, wherein an outer surface of each of the first and second tab portions define a horizontally extending groove. 26. The mounting clip of claim 21, wherein the coupler comprises an elongate channel and provides an interference fit with the portion of the network cable residing in the channel. 27. The mounting clip of claim 21, wherein the coupler comprises a resilient ring member that can be deformed to open configuration to permit the portion of the network 

 cable to be moved into the interior space of the ring member and returns to a closed configuration with the ring member partly encircling the portion of the network cable in the closed configuration. 28. The mounting clip of claim 21, wherein the coupler comprises at least one pair of spaced resilient fingers extending downwardly from the mid-portion and defining an interior region between the fingers sized to receive the portion of the network cable. 29. A mounting clip for use in securing a portion of a network cable to a planar support, the mounting clip comprising: a planar mid-portion having a first surface adapted to be flush mounted against the planar support and a second surface on the opposite side of the mid-portion; and a coupler disposed adjacent the second surface of the mid-portion and configured to releasably secure the portion of the network cable to the mounting clip. 30. The mounting clip according to claim 29, wherein the mid-portion includes an aperture for receiving a fastener for coupling the mounting clip to the support. 31. The mounting clip according to claim 29, wherein the support is a metal support and mid-portion includes one or more integral magnets for magnetically coupling the mounting clip to the support. 32. The mounting clip of claim 29, wherein the coupler comprises an elongate channel and provides an interface fit with the portion of the network cable residing in the channel. 33. The mounting clip of claim 29, wherein the coupler comprises a resilient ring member that can be deformed to open configuration to permit the portion of the network cable to be moved into the interior space of the ring member and returns to a closed position with the ring member partly encircling the portion of the network cable in the closed position. 34. The mounting clip of claim 29, wherein the coupler comprises at least one pair of spaced resilient fingers extending downwardly from the mid-portion and defining an interior region between the fingers sized to receive the portion of the network cable.
